title: asperula galioides subsp. tyraica
intro: Asperula galioides subsp. tyraica, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of phenolic compounds and essential oils. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Woodruff can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the fresh roots and leaves being used in traditional medicine.
